questions.question: What is an election?
questions.answer: An election is a process through which people choose their representatives.

questions.question: What is democracy?
questions.answer: Democracy is a system of government where people elect their representatives.

questions.question: Why are elections necessary in a democracy?
questions.answer: Elections allow people to choose and change their representatives.

questions.question: What is political competition?
questions.answer: Political competition is the contest among parties and candidates to win elections.

questions.question: What is a constituency?
questions.answer: A constituency is a geographical area that elects one representative.

questions.question: What is the voters list?
questions.answer: The voters list is the official list of eligible voters in a constituency.

questions.question: What is universal adult franchise?
questions.answer: Universal adult franchise means all citizens aged 18 and above can vote.

questions.question: What is an electoral roll?
questions.answer: An electoral roll is another name for the voters list.

questions.question: Who conducts elections in India?
questions.answer: The Election Commission of India conducts elections.

questions.question: What is an election campaign?
questions.answer: An election campaign is when candidates promote their policies to voters.

questions.question: What is polling?
questions.answer: Polling is the process of casting votes during an election.

questions.question: What machine is used for voting in India?
questions.answer: Electronic Voting Machine (EVM) is used for voting.

questions.question: What are reserved constituencies?
questions.answer: Reserved constituencies are seats reserved for SC and ST candidates.

questions.question: What does MP stand for?
questions.answer: MP stands for Member of Parliament.

questions.question: What does MLA stand for?
questions.answer: MLA stands for Member of Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: What is a by-election?
questions.answer: A by-election is an election held to fill a vacant seat.

questions.question: What is a general election?
questions.answer: A general election is held to elect representatives for all constituencies.

questions.question: What is the minimum voting age in India?
questions.answer: The minimum voting age in India is 18 years.

questions.question: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
questions.answer: It is a set of rules for political parties during elections.

questions.question: What is the main function of the Election Commission?
questions.answer: To conduct free and fair elections.

questions.question: What happens after votes are counted?
questions.answer: The candidate with the highest votes is declared the winner.

questions.question: What is vote counting?
questions.answer: Vote counting is the process of counting the votes cast in an election.

questions.question: Why are elections held regularly?
questions.answer: Elections are held regularly to ensure accountability of leaders.

questions.question: Who prepares the voters list?
questions.answer: The government prepares the voters list under the supervision of the Election Commission.

questions.question: What ensures free and fair elections in India?
questions.answer: The independent Election Commission ensures free and fair elections.
